behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "direct and transitive dependencies ... included in the vulnerability check" requires a module-boundary handoff (manifest parsing/resolution -> scan input), not just one pure function. Unit is too low because real failures often appear when resolver output is incorrectly passed to the query layer. Rejected System because the behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; an in-process resolver + fake vuln client can expose the failure. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/LLM step is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner with fixtures containing direct and transitive deps (including edge cases like nested/aliased deps), using a fake vulnerability client that records requested packages/versions; assert every resolved dependency is present in outgoing check set with no omissions/duplicates.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "match the exact version ranges ... not just the package name" points to correctness at the query-construction + response-filtering boundary between modules. Unit is too low if query builder and API adapter interact incorrectly. Rejected System: no explicit requirement for real HTTP/network behavior; a controlled fake service can catch malformed range filters. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire query-builder and API client against a deterministic fake vuln DB endpoint that differentiates by version range; assert scanner only returns CVEs for manifest-matching ranges and excludes same-package/non-matching versions.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure ordering logic: "Critical above High above Medium above Low ... same severity sorted by CVSS score." No required module handoff, real infra, or AI dependency is stated. Higher levels are unnecessary for this failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Test the sorter function with mixed severities/CVSS ties and assert strict order by severity rank then descending CVSS within each severity bucket.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged" is a parse/transform/serialize boundary across modules where regressions often occur (formatting, key reordering, unintended edits). Unit can miss cross-module mutation drift. Rejected System because real OS/network/subprocess is not required to detect this; local file/AST pipeline is sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest update pipeline on representative manifests (including comments/order/whitespace-sensitive formats where applicable), then diff before/after and assert exactly one dependency version change and byte-for-byte stability of unrelated entries.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "created on the real repository" explicitly requires real external API/repo behavior (git hosting, branch/PR creation, remote diff rendering). Mocking can hide auth, permission, branch protection, and diff computation mismatches. Rejected Integration because in-process fakes cannot fully reproduce real repo/API failure modes. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no LLM-dependent decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test repository, execute scanner remediation flow for one vulnerable dependency; assert a PR is opened remotely, branch exists, and PR diff contains exactly one dependency-version change with no unrelated file modifications.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is isolated rule evaluation: "major version bump ... breaking change based on semver comparison." No module interaction or real infra is required to catch core failures.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver decision function across normal and edge cases (pre-release tags, 0.x semantics per project policy, build metadata) and assert breaking-change flag only when rules indicate major incompatibility.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced" requires orchestration/state handoff across detector -> PR service -> reporting modules. Unit is too low because failure appears at interaction boundaries (e.g., flag set but PR still attempted). Rejected System because this can be verified with a stub PR client call-capture and integrated output module; no real network/OS is required by the behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model decision in loop.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end scanner flow in-process with a stub PR provider and output collector for a known breaking bump; assert PR provider is never called and scanner output includes the review-required flag/reason.
